message:
Move cache directory to user-isolated path, add 24h hard timeout for stuck builds, and use epoch-based build IDs

- Replace hardcoded /tmp/rch cache base with per-user directory using
  dirs::cache_dir() (~/.cache/rch) to prevent symlink attacks and cache
  collisions on shared /tmp partitions; fall back to /tmp/rch-$USER if
  dirs::cache_dir() is unavailable; update all cache path tests to use
  path-contains assertions instead of exact path equality
- Add 24-hour hard timeout to stuck build remediation that triggers
  cleanup regardless of other evidence signals (slot ownership, worker
  binding, heartbeat staleness), ensuring builds cannot be stuck
  indefinitely even if some signals are ambiguous
- Generate epoch-based build IDs by left-shifting the current Unix
  timestamp by 24 bits, preventing ID collisions with orphaned remote
  processes when the daemon crashes and restarts with a fresh counter

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
